attorney-general-v-32000-in-canadian-cur.lesson: Pro-se filer warned after fictitious citations and non-response about AI use; no consequences imposed but future repeats threatened.

noura-ahmed-v-troy-powell-peggy-pulliam-.lesson: Self-represented applicant cited non-existent LRB decision; board simply disregarded it without sanction.

zahariev-v-zaharieva-2025-06-09.lesson: Pro-se petitioner cited multiple fabricated and misrepresented cases; court rejected propositions on verification failure.

rodney-chagas-v-fabricio-petinelli-vieir.lesson: Brazilian appellate fined lawyer 1% of case value for AI-fabricated jurisprudence; trust principle does not excuse gross error.

chen-v-vana-et-al-2025-06-08.lesson: Delegated AI drafting to a staffer with no verification; lawyer fined personally and warned dismissal was an option.

ayinde-v-haringey-al-haroun-v-qnb-2025-0.lesson: UK High Court issued Hamid guidance referring lawyers to professional bodies; supervision and training failures invite discipline even without contempt.

lakhanpal-v-avis-budget-group-inc-2025-0.lesson: Tribunal gave no weight to three fabricated HRTO citations from self-represented applicant; no penalty imposed.

ivins-v-kma-consulting-engineers-ors-202.lesson: Self-represented complainant escaped sanction for fabricated QIRC cites; commission expressly noted a lawyer would face misconduct referral.

ploni-v-wasserman-et-al-2025-06-01.lesson: Pro-se litigant fined 250 ILS for wasting court time with nonexistent authorities generated by ChatGPT.

ko-v-li-2025-05-28.lesson: Counsel escaped fine only by agreeing to CPD and forgoing billing; ChatGPT hallucinations trigger mandatory training orders.

gnx-v-childrens-guardian-2025-05-27.lesson: Pro-se applicant warned after ChatGPT misdescribed case law; court urged retaining counsel rather than relying on chatbots.

r-v-chand-2025-05-26.lesson: Ontario court warned counsel and directed AI-use protocols for the remainder of a criminal case after misrepresented precedent surfaced.

so-and-so-v-v-anonymous-2025-05-26.lesson: Israeli courts flagged fabricated cites but declined sanctions this time; next uncontrolled AI use will draw discipline under Supreme Court guidance.

mahala-association-מהלה-v-clalit-health-.lesson: Paid 'Takdin AI' tool did not save counsel from 50,000 ILS fine, stricken class action, and personal disqualification; vetted tools demand human verification.

shakori-v-tern-2025-onltb-31233-2025-05-.lesson: Pro-se tenant's ChatGPT-sourced citations rejected as fictitious; admitted post-hearing but no sanction imposed.

vechtel-et-al-v-gershoni-2025-05-25.lesson: Motion denied where pro-se plaintiffs invented a quote from the presiding judge's own prior judgment.

nikolic-anor-v-nationwide-news-pty-ltd-a.lesson: Victoria Supreme Court treated three AI-fabricated authorities from pro-se plaintiffs as non-existent; no sanction but credibility destroyed.

ehrlich-v-israel-national-academy-of-sci.lesson: Pro-se applicant fined 500 ILS; courts reject the 'good-faith AI mistake' defense regardless of representation status.

fox-v-assum-2025-05-14.lesson: Israeli court ordered 1,200 ILS costs and demanded formal explanation for imaginary judgment; unverified AI drafting now triggers show-cause orders.

department-of-justice-v-wise-2025-05-13.lesson: Pro-se respondent repeated fabricated citations after prior warning; QCAT relied on non-lawyer AI guidelines to caution rather than sanction.
